task_completion.description: Verify the location is set to the US Naval Observatory coordinates.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Stellarium's Location window is open showing Latitude around 38.92 N (or 38°55') and Longitude around 77.06 W (or 77°04'), or the location name is explicitly set to US Naval Observatory.

task_completion.description: Verify the simulation date is July 4, 1876, and the time zone is UTC.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Stellarium's Date and Time window or the bottom status bar shows the date as 1876-07-04 and the time zone as UTC.

task_completion.description: Verify the star Vega is selected in the simulation.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Vega is targeted with crosshairs in the Stellarium sky view, and its information block (name, magnitude, coordinates) is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Verify the simulation time is aligned to Vega's exact transit time.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The simulation time is between 04:45 and 04:59 UTC (or 05:00-05:05 UTC), and Vega is positioned exactly on the meridian (highest point in the sky, azimuth exactly 180° South or 0° North, or transit time in the info panel matches the current simulation time).

task_completion.description: Verify a screenshot of the transit was saved to the correct directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A notification in Stellarium indicating a screenshot was saved, or a file manager/terminal showing a newly created image file inside the ~/Pictures/stellarium/ directory.

task_completion.description: Verify the log file was created with the correct details.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal displaying the contents of ~/Desktop/transit_log.txt, containing the star name ('Vega'), date ('1876-07-04'), and the observed transit time (e.g., '04:50').

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the Stellarium GUI to find the information.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show the Stellarium application open, with the agent navigating its menus and sky view.

integrity.description: Verify the agent actually aligned the sky to the transit time in Stellarium.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Stellarium sky view actively reflecting the 1876-07-04 transit time with Vega on the meridian, rather than the agent just typing the expected answer into a text file without changing the sky.

integrity.description: Verify the agent didn't use an external script to bypass the Stellarium GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no evidence of the agent writing or running Python scripts (using libraries like AstroPy or PyEphem) in the terminal to calculate the transit time instead of observing it in Stellarium.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target location is 38.9215 N, 77.0669 W (US Naval Observatory). The target date is 1876-07-04. The target star is Vega. The expected UTC transit time for Vega on this date and location is approximately 04:50 UTC (acceptable range is 04:45-04:59 UTC, or alternatively 05:00-05:05 UTC depending on Stellarium's Delta T settings). The log file must be saved at ~/Desktop/transit_log.txt and the screenshot must be saved in ~/Pictures/stellarium/.
